task_completion.description: Navigate to the Inventory section in HospitalRun
task_completion.visual_evidence: The HospitalRun application is visible with the 'Inventory' section selected in the navigation menu or the main view displaying the inventory list.
The item 'Amoxicillin 500mg' is visible in the inventory list or search results.

task_completion.description: Open the edit view for the target item
task_completion.visual_evidence: An edit form or modal is open specifically for the item 'Amoxicillin 500mg'.
The input field for 'Reorder Point' (or equivalent safety stock setting) contains the value '150'.
The input field for 'Price' contains the value '12.50'.
A success message (e.g., 'Saved', 'Updated') is visible, or the view returns to the inventory list showing the updated values for 'Amoxicillin 500mg', or the save button is shown being clicked.

integrity.description: Agent used the HospitalRun GUI, not terminal commands or direct database queries
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows, curl commands, or direct CouchDB interactions are visible in the screenshots.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual HospitalRun web application
integrity.visual_evidence: The HospitalRun interface is visible in a standard web browser window, not a text editor or a mock HTML file.

integrity.description: Agent entered the values into the correct form fields
integrity.visual_evidence: The values '150' and '12.50' are typed directly into the standard input fields of the HospitalRun edit form, rather than being pasted into a search bar or unrelated text area.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target item is 'Amoxicillin 500mg'. Its initial reorder point is 50 and initial price is 10.00. The agent must update the reorder point to exactly 150 and the price to exactly 12.50 using the HospitalRun web interface.
